Dashboard Archive: 2026-04-23 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
Today's momentum ranker shows ERAS dominating with a rank-1 score of 98.3, driven by extraordinary long-term returns (1528% over one year, 844% over six months) while maintaining exceptional consistency across all timeframes and passing all SMA gates. SITM ranks second with a 326% one-year return and strong near-term momentum (56.94% over one month), though its three-month return of 53.32% shows some deceleration compared to its monthly gains, suggesting recent momentum may be cooling relative to ERAS's sustained acceleration. Both tickers pass all technical gates and ratio consistency checks, but ERAS's resilience score (99.68 on bad SPY days) significantly outpaces SITM's (0.58), indicating ERAS maintained strength even when the broader market declined while SITM was more vulnerable. The data reveals a two-tier pattern: ERAS exhibits multi-year compounding with remarkably stable ratio progression, whereas SITM displays stronger near-term acceleration but weaker medium-term sustainability and market-relative protection.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
